description: city in Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Newton is a city in Middlesex County, Massachusetts, United States.

description: neighborhood in Boston, Massachusetts, United States of America
description_wiki: The South End is a neighborhood in Boston, Massachusetts, United States which is bordered by Back Bay, Chinatown, and Roxbury.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: naval training base for the United States Navy, outside of Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: Naval Station Great Lakes (NAVSTA Great Lakes) is the home of the United States Navy's only current boot camp, located near North Chicago, in Lake County, Illinois, along Lake Michigan.

description: undergraduate college of Harvard University in Cambridge, Massachusetts
description_wiki: Harvard College is the undergraduate college within the Faculty of Arts and Sciences at Harvard University.

description: commencement Bay-class escort carrier of the US Navy
description_wiki: USS Puget Sound was a Commencement Bay-class escort carrier of the United States Navy.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Alameda (  AL-ə-MEE-də; Spanish: [alaˈmeða]; Spanish for "tree-lined path") is a city in Alameda County, California, United States, located in the East Bay region of the Bay Area.

aliases: Queen City of the Pacific and others
description: capital city of the Philippines
description_wiki: Manila, officially the City of Manila, is the capital and second-most populous city of the Philippines after Quezon City.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

description: multirole combat aircraft family
description_wiki: The Lockheed P-80 Shooting Star is an early jet fighter that was designed and built by Lockheed.

description: core city in Kyushu, Japan
description_wiki: Nagasaki (長崎, Nagasaki; Japanese pronunciation: [na.ɡaꜜ.sa.kʲi, na.ŋa-], lit. 'Long Cape'), officially Nagasaki City (長崎市, Nagasaki-shi; [na.ɡa.sa.kʲi̥ꜜ.ɕi, na.ɡa.saꜜ.kʲi̥.ɕi, na.ŋa-]), is the capital and the largest city of Nagasaki Prefecture on the island of Kyushu in Japan.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: bombed Nagasaki and Japan with the atomic bomb
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

aliases: Agenzia delle Entrate – Ministero dell’Economia e delle Finanze
Agenzia delle Entrate Ministero dell'Economia e delle Finanze
description: revenue service of the United States federal government
description_wiki: The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is the revenue service of the U.S. federal government, which is responsible for collecting U.S. federal taxes and administering the Internal Revenue Code, the main body of the federal statutory tax law.

description: scholarly article in Journal of Sichuan Normal University (Social Sciences Edition), no. 1, 2017
first public school in the United States
description_wiki: The Boston Latin School is a magnet Latin grammar state school in Boston, Massachusetts.

description: imaginary line that demarcates the change of one calendar day to the next
description_wiki: The International Date Line (IDL) is the line extending between the South and North Poles that is the boundary between one calendar day and the next.

description: 1944 cruise missile by Fieseler
description_wiki: The V-1 flying bomb (German: Vergeltungswaffe 1, lit. 'Vengeance Weapon 1') was an early cruise missile.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity
description_wiki: Methodism, also called the Methodist movement, is a Protestant Christian tradition whose origins, doctrines and practice derive from the life and teachings of John Wesley.

description: capital city of Illinois and county seat of Sangamon County
description_wiki: Springfield is the capital city of the U.S. state of Illinois.
